name: Sweet Corn and Cheddar Chowder
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Corn kernels, fresh or frozen
3 cups Half and half or milk
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt 3 tablespoons of butter in a heavy large saucepan over medium heat.
2. Add the chopped onion, celery, and carrot to the pan. Sauté until the vegetables begin to soften, about 5 minutes.
3. Stir in 2 teaspoons dry mustard.
4. Add the canned chicken broth, potatoes, bay leaf, and thyme to the pan. Bring the mixture to a boil.
5. Reduce heat to low, cover the saucepan, and simmer until the potatoes are almost tender, about 10 minutes.
6. Stir in the corn and half-and-half. Return the soup to a simmer.
7. Cover the saucepan and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 6 minutes.
8. Lower the heat to very low and gradually stir in the cheddar cheese until it is just melted into the soup.
9. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
10. Ladle the soup into deep bowls, garnish with remaining cheese and green onion, and serve.
